attractions.name: Alaska Native Heritage Center
attractions.season: Daily mid-May to mid-Sept; sharply reduced winter/event-only hours
attractions.blurb: Recreated village sites from all 11 Alaska Native culture groups around a lake, plus dance, storytelling and artist demos.
attractions.tip: Arrive by 10am for the first dance set, then walk the lake loop; combo ticket with Anchorage Museum saves money.
attractions.duration_note: half-day from downtown Anchorage; free summer shuttle

attractions.name: Anchorage Museum at Rasmuson Center
attractions.aka: Smithsonian Arctic Studies Center Alaska office
attractions.season: Year-round; daily in summer, closed Mondays Oct-Apr
attractions.blurb: Alaska's largest museum: state history galleries, planetarium, and the Smithsonian Arctic Studies Center's 600 Native objects.
attractions.tip: The Smithsonian gallery on the 2nd floor is the real reason to come; free entry on first-Friday evenings.
attractions.duration_note: best rainy-day stop in Anchorage

attractions.name: Eklutna Historical Park
attractions.season: Guided tours daily late May to early Sept; grounds viewable year-round
attractions.blurb: Dena'ina Athabascan cemetery of brightly painted wooden spirit houses beside an 1870s Russian Orthodox log church.
attractions.tip: Mile 26 Glenn Hwy, 25 min north of Anchorage; pair with Thunderbird Falls trailhead two miles away.
attractions.duration_note: quick stop driving north out of Anchorage

attractions.name: Earthquake Park
attractions.aka: 1964 Good Friday earthquake site, Turnagain Heights slide
attractions.season: Year-round; trails snow-covered Nov-Apr
attractions.blurb: Hummocky woods where the 1964 M9.2 quake slid the Turnagain Heights neighborhood toward Cook Inlet; interpretive panels.
attractions.tip: The slide scars are subtle and overgrown, so read the panels first; strong Denali view from the Coastal Trail here.
attractions.duration_note: 30-60 min on the Tony Knowles Coastal Trail

attractions.name: Alaska Federation of Natives Convention & Elders and Youth
attractions.aka: AFN; First Alaskans Elders & Youth Conference
attractions.season: Mid-to-late October; Elders & Youth runs the three days before AFN
attractions.blurb: Largest annual gathering of Alaska Native people: thousands of delegates, dance groups and a huge craft market.
attractions.tip: Venue rotates between Anchorage's Dena'ina Center and Fairbanks by year; hotels sell out months ahead.
attractions.duration_note: 3-6 day event; drop in for a day

attractions.name: Iditarod Trail Sled Dog Race Headquarters
attractions.season: Daily 8-7 in summer; weekdays only and shorter hours Oct-Apr
attractions.cost: Free entry; about $15 for a wheeled sled-dog ride
attractions.blurb: Race museum with Joe Redington memorabilia, retired sleds and champion gear, plus summer wheeled dog-cart rides.
attractions.tip: Mile 2.2 Knik-Goose Bay Rd, easy detour en route to Denali; the real race restart is at Willow each March.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2 hrs off the Parks Highway

attractions.name: Independence Mine State Historical Park
attractions.season: Mine road and visitor center open roughly mid-June to early Sept; upper Hatcher Pass Rd snow-closed Oct-June
attractions.blurb: Ruins of a 1930s hard-rock gold camp at 3,500 ft in Hatcher Pass: bunkhouses, mill and assay office on alpine tundra.
attractions.tip: Last two miles are steep gravel; visitor center in the manager's house opens late morning. Superb evening light.
attractions.duration_note: half-day from Palmer or Anchorage

attractions.name: Talkeetna Historical Society Museum
attractions.aka: Talkeetna Museum; old Moose Dropping Festival organizer
attractions.season: Daily 9-5 through Sept 15; Fri-Sat 11-5 in winter or by appointment
attractions.blurb: 1936 one-room schoolhouse of Talkeetna and Denali-climbing history, with a large relief model used by mountaineering rangers.
attractions.tip: The Moose Dropping Festival it once ran ended in 2009; December's Wilderness Woman contest is the surviving oddity.
attractions.duration_note: 45 min in downtown Talkeetna

attractions.name: University of Alaska Museum of the North
attractions.season: Daily 9-7 in summer; Mon-Sat 9-5 the rest of the year
attractions.blurb: Alaska's best combined natural-history and Native art collection: Blue Babe the mummified steppe bison, mammoths, aurora.
attractions.tip: Summer-only aurora and Native-games films run hourly upstairs; the ridge-top building and Alaska Range view are free.
attractions.duration_note: 2-3 hrs on the UAF campus west of town

attractions.name: Morris Thompson Cultural and Visitors Center
attractions.aka: Fairbanks visitor center; Alaska Public Lands Information Center
attractions.season: Open year-round; long summer hours, shorter in winter
attractions.blurb: Free Interior Alaska exhibit hall plus Public Lands Info Center on the Chena riverfront; trip-planning desk for Arctic travel.
attractions.tip: Best free stop in Fairbanks: summer Native artist demos and daily films, and staff who know Dalton Hwy conditions.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2 hrs; walkable from downtown hotels

attractions.name: Pioneer Park
attractions.season: Grounds open year-round; museums, shops and rides late May to early Sept
attractions.cost: Free entry; small fees for museums and rides
attractions.blurb: 44-acre historic park of relocated gold-rush cabins, the sternwheeler Nenana, and aviation, railroad and Native museums.
attractions.tip: Free to walk in; the Nenana riverboat and Pioneer Air Museum charge separately. Salmon Bake dinner nightly in summer.
attractions.duration_note: 2-3 hrs, easy evening activity in midnight sun

attractions.name: Riverboat Discovery & Chena Indian Village
attractions.season: Mid-May to mid-Sept, two departures daily (morning and afternoon)
attractions.blurb: 3.5-hr sternwheeler on the Chena with a walking stop at a staffed Athabascan village: fish camp, sled dogs, furs, beadwork.
attractions.tip: Sells out in July, so reserve ahead; the run includes a bush-plane demo and Susan Butcher's sled-dog kennel.
attractions.duration_note: half-day; dock is off Dale Rd near the airport

attractions.name: Kennecott Mines National Historic Landmark
attractions.aka: Kennicott; Wrangell-St. Elias copper mill town
attractions.season: Shuttles, lodge and guided mill tours late May to early Sept; road passable but unserviced in winter
attractions.cost: Free to walk the town; about $32 for the mill tour
attractions.blurb: Blood-red 14-story copper mill and company town abandoned in 1938 inside Wrangell-St. Elias NP, stabilized by the NPS.
attractions.tip: 60-mi gravel McCarthy Rd takes 2.5 hrs; park at the footbridge and shuttle 5 mi. Mill interior only on a guided tour.
attractions.duration_note: realistically an overnight in McCarthy/Kennecott

attractions.name: Old Valdez Townsite Memorial
attractions.season: Road-accessible year-round
attractions.blurb: Empty street grid four miles east of town where the 1964 quake and its wave destroyed old Valdez and killed 32 on the dock.
attractions.tip: Only street signs and a memorial remain; pair it with the Valdez Museum's Remembering Old Valdez model annex.
attractions.duration_note: 20-30 min pullout on the Richardson Hwy

attractions.name: Klondike Gold Rush National Historical Park
attractions.season: Visitor center and ranger programs early May to late Sept; most historic buildings closed in winter
attractions.blurb: Restored 1898 boomtown blocks on Broadway run as a national park: Mascot Saloon, Jeff Smith's Parlor, Moore Homestead.
attractions.tip: Free ranger walking tours leave the 2nd & Broadway depot several times daily; go early before the cruise ships unload.
attractions.duration_note: half-day walking downtown Skagway

attractions.name: Chilkoot Trail
attractions.season: Practical hiking window early July to early Sept; permits required in the summer season
attractions.cost: Permits required, roughly $60-100 total US + Canada
attractions.blurb: 33-mi stampeder route from Dyea over 3,525-ft Chilkoot Pass to Bennett BC, still littered with abandoned 1898 gear.
attractions.tip: Reserve Parks Canada permits from January; exit by WP&YR train at Bennett and clear customs. Golden Stairs is loose scree.
attractions.duration_note: 3-5 days; part of the Dyea townsite trail network is closed by river erosion

attractions.name: Alaska State Museum
attractions.aka: APK, Father Andrew P. Kashevaroff Building
attractions.season: Daily mid-May to late Sept; Tue-Sat the rest of the year
attractions.blurb: Statewide collection in one modern building: Tlingit and Haida art, Russian-America relics, and a two-story eagle nesting tree.
attractions.tip: Shares the APK building with the State Library and Archives; your ticket also covers the Sheldon Jackson Museum in Sitka.
attractions.duration_note: 2 hrs, 15-min walk from the Juneau cruise docks

attractions.name: Sealaska Heritage Walter Soboleff Building
attractions.aka: Sealaska Heritage Institute; SHI Arts Campus
attractions.season: Daily in summer; weekdays the rest of the year
attractions.blurb: Tlingit, Haida and Tsimshian cultural center built around Shuka Hit, a full clan house of hand-carved red cedar screens.
attractions.tip: Lobby glass screen and outdoor totems are free; the Arts Campus a block away has carvers working outdoors in summer.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2 hrs in downtown Juneau

attractions.name: Celebration (Sealaska Heritage)
attractions.season: Biennial, even-numbered years, four days in early-to-mid June
attractions.blurb: Largest Southeast Native gathering: 2,000+ dancers from 50+ Tlingit, Haida and Tsimshian groups plus a downtown grand parade.
attractions.tip: Even years only, so the next is June 2028; Juneau lodging books out. The opening parade is free to watch.
attractions.duration_note: 4-day event centered on Centennial Hall

attractions.name: Tahku the Whale Project
attractions.season: Year-round; fountain runs roughly May-Sept
attractions.blurb: Life-size bronze breaching humpback in a fountain pool on the Gastineau Channel waterfront; a statehood-anniversary work.
attractions.tip: At Overstreet Park under the Juneau-Douglas bridge, a 10-min walk south of the cruise docks; jets look best midday.
attractions.duration_note: 15-30 min photo stop on the seawalk

attractions.name: Huna Tribal House (Xunaa Shuka Hit)
attractions.aka: Glacier Bay tribal house, Bartlett Cove
attractions.season: Late May to early Sept, with Huna culture-bearer programs most days
attractions.blurb: Huna Tlingit clan house at Glacier Bay park headquarters, opened 2016: carved house screens, totems and daily culture talks.
attractions.tip: Five-minute walk from Glacier Bay Lodge; day-cruise passengers never land, so you need a Gustavus overnight to see it.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2 hrs; most reach Gustavus by air from Juneau

attractions.name: Icy Strait Point
attractions.aka: Hoonah cannery, Huna Totem Corporation port
attractions.season: Cruise season May-Sept only; largely shut down in winter
attractions.cost: Free to walk; gondola, zipline and tours extra
attractions.blurb: Alaska Native-owned cruise port in a 1912 salmon cannery: Tlingit dance shows, cannery museum, gondolas, huge zipline.
attractions.tip: Only alive on ship days. Bear-viewing and whale tours sell out; the town of Hoonah is a 1.5-mi walk east.
attractions.duration_note: a full cruise port-day

attractions.name: Jilkaat Kwaan Heritage Center
attractions.season: Tue-Sat mid-May to mid-Sept; winter by appointment
attractions.blurb: Tlingit-run center in Klukwan village displaying the four Whale House house-posts and Rain Screen, carvings roughly 200 years old.
attractions.tip: Mile 22 Haines Hwy inside the Chilkat Bald Eagle Preserve; call ahead since hours follow the tour-bus schedule.
attractions.duration_note: half-day round trip from Haines

attractions.name: Sitka National Historical Park
attractions.aka: Totem Park; 1804 Battle of Sitka site
attractions.season: Trails open year-round; visitor center daily May-Sept, reduced in winter
attractions.blurb: Alaska's oldest federal park: a 1-mi rainforest trail lined with Tlingit and Haida totems to the 1804 Tlingit fort site.
attractions.tip: Carvers work in the Southeast Alaska Indian Cultural Center inside the visitor center; salmon fill Indian River in August.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2 hrs, walkable from downtown Sitka

attractions.name: Sheldon Jackson Museum
attractions.season: Daily 9-5 mid-May to mid-Sept; Tue-Sat 10-4 in winter
attractions.blurb: Alaska's oldest museum, an 1897 concrete octagon holding Sheldon Jackson's Native collection: kayaks, masks, tools, regalia.
attractions.tip: Small room, but the densest Alaska Native artifact display in the state; your Alaska State Museum ticket covers it.
attractions.duration_note: 1 hr, next door to Sitka NHP

attractions.name: Russian Bishop's House
attractions.season: Daily ranger tours May-Sept; winter by appointment
attractions.blurb: 1842 log residence of Bishop Innocent, one of only four surviving Russian-colonial buildings in North America; NPS-restored.
attractions.tip: Ground floor is free; the upstairs chapel and living quarters are ranger-guided only, roughly hourly in summer.
attractions.duration_note: 45 min on Lincoln St between town and Totem Park

attractions.name: St. Michael's Cathedral
attractions.aka: Cathedral of St. Michael the Archangel, Sitka
attractions.season: Interior open on cruise days and around services, mainly May-Sept
attractions.blurb: 1848 Russian Orthodox cathedral rebuilt after a 1966 fire; icons including the Sitka Madonna were rescued and hang inside.
attractions.tip: It stands in the middle of Lincoln Street, unmissable. Interior hours are short and irregular, so check the door sign.
attractions.duration_note: 20-30 min stop in downtown Sitka

attractions.name: Chief Shakes Island & Tribal House
attractions.season: Island and totems year-round; clan house interior on cruise days or by arrangement
attractions.cost: Free grounds; about $5 for the house tour
attractions.blurb: Footbridge islet in Wrangell's harbor with a Tlingit clan house rebuilt in 2013 and six carved house-post totems.
attractions.tip: Grounds are always open and free; combine with Petroglyph Beach a mile north, which only works at low tide.
attractions.duration_note: 45 min; Wrangell is an easy AMHS ferry stop

attractions.name: Totem Bight State Historical Park
attractions.season: Open dawn to dusk year-round; shelter staffed in summer
attractions.blurb: Fourteen restored and recreated Tlingit and Haida totems on a rainforest loop, plus a painted clan house on the beach.
attractions.tip: Mile 10 N Tongass Hwy, 20 min from downtown Ketchikan; quietest before 10am, then tour buses arrive in waves.
attractions.duration_note: 1 hr plus 40 min of driving round trip

attractions.name: Saxman Native Village & Totem Park
attractions.season: Village tours on cruise-ship days May-Sept; totem park open year-round
attractions.cost: Free to walk the poles; about $50 for the village tour
attractions.blurb: Largest standing collection of totem poles in the world, roughly 25, in a Tlingit village with a carving shed and clan house.
attractions.tip: Free to wander the poles anytime; the Beaver Clan House dance show and carver talk only run for booked tour groups.
attractions.duration_note: 2 hrs; 2.5 mi south of Ketchikan

attractions.name: Totem Heritage Center
attractions.season: Daily in summer; limited weekday afternoons Oct-Apr
attractions.blurb: Thirty-plus unrestored 19th-century totem poles retrieved from abandoned Tlingit and Haida villages, kept indoors as found.
attractions.tip: The only place to see original weathered poles instead of replicas; 15-min uphill walk from the cruise docks.
attractions.duration_note: 1 hr; pair with Creek Street on the walk back

attractions.name: Blueberry Arts Festival
attractions.season: First full weekend of August, two days
attractions.blurb: Ketchikan's long-running downtown street fair: 100+ art booths, slug races, poetry slam and pie-eating, all blueberry-themed.
attractions.tip: Run by the Ketchikan Area Arts & Humanities Council; downtown streets close, so walk in from the ships or the tunnel.
attractions.duration_note: half-day; overlaps peak cruise season

attractions.name: Baranov Museum
attractions.season: Tue-Sat year-round, longer hours in summer
attractions.blurb: An 1808 Russian-American Company fur warehouse, the oldest Russian-built structure in the US, now Kodiak's history museum.
attractions.tip: Recently renovated and sits right above the harbor, so it fits neatly into a Tustumena ferry layover.
attractions.duration_note: 1 hr in downtown Kodiak

attractions.name: Alutiiq Museum & Archaeological Repository
attractions.season: Tue-Sat year-round
attractions.blurb: Alutiiq/Sugpiaq-run museum holding 250,000 artifacts from 7,500 years on Kodiak, plus active language and dance revival work.
attractions.tip: Small gallery but a serious collection; ask about the season's digs, and the shop is the best source of real Alutiiq art.
attractions.duration_note: 1 hr, two blocks from the Baranov Museum

attractions.name: Holy Resurrection Russian Orthodox Cathedral
attractions.aka: Kodiak Orthodox cathedral, St. Herman relics
attractions.season: Year-round; interior open around services or by arrangement
attractions.blurb: Blue-domed 1940s church of the oldest Russian Orthodox parish in the Americas, founded 1794; relics of St. Herman inside.
attractions.tip: Saturday vespers and Sunday liturgy are the reliable times to get in; photography is discouraged during services.
attractions.duration_note: 20 min stop on the downtown walk

attractions.name: Aleutian Islands WWII National Historic Area Visitor Center
attractions.season: Staffed Tue-Sat in summer; very limited or appointment-only in winter
attractions.blurb: NPS museum in a 1940s naval air-transport building covering the Aleutian campaign, the Attu evacuation and Unangax internment.
attractions.tip: At 2716 Airport Beach Rd by the airport; then drive up Bunker Hill and Fort Schwatka for the concrete gun emplacements.
attractions.duration_note: 2 hrs; the Museum of the Aleutians in town complements it

attractions.name: Attu Island Battlefield
attractions.aka: Battle of Attu NHL; Kiska Island lies 180 mi east
attractions.season: No regular access; rare chartered expedition landings June-Aug
attractions.cost: Charter or expedition cruise only, very expensive
attractions.blurb: Site of the only WWII land battle fought on North American soil, May 1943; now uninhabited tundra at the end of the chain.
attractions.tip: The Coast Guard LORAN station closed in 2010, so there are no services; Kiska has the better-preserved Japanese wrecks.
attractions.duration_note: multi-day expedition; realistically a map pin, not an itinerary stop

attractions.name: Inupiat Heritage Center
attractions.season: Weekdays year-round; closes for whaling festivals in late June
attractions.blurb: NPS-affiliated Inupiaq museum on bowhead whaling, umiaks and the Arctic whaling trade, partnered with New Bedford Whaling NHP.
attractions.tip: Reachable only by jet from Anchorage or Fairbanks; call ahead because it shuts for Nalukataq whaling feasts in June.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2 hrs; core of any Utqiagvik day trip
